question: Change to Indirect: Ram said, 'I am busy.'
options: Ram said that he was busy.
Ram said that I am busy.
Ram says that he is busy.
Ram said he is busy.
answer: Ram said that he was busy.
explanation: Simple Present 'am' changes to Simple Past 'was' when the reporting verb 'said' is in the past. ✅

question: Change to Indirect: She says, 'I am eating an apple.'
options: She says that she was eating an apple.
She says that she is eating an apple.
She said that she is eating an apple.
She says that I am eating an apple.
answer: She says that she is eating an apple.
explanation: If the reporting verb is in the Present Tense (says), the tense of the reported speech DOES NOT change. ✅

question: Change to Indirect: He said to me, 'I will meet you tomorrow.'
options: He told me that he would meet me tomorrow.
He told me that he would meet me the next day.
He said to me that he will meet me the next day.
He told me that I would meet him tomorrow.
answer: He told me that he would meet me the next day.
explanation: 'Said to' becomes 'told', 'will' becomes 'would', and 'tomorrow' changes to 'the next day'. ✅

question: Change to Indirect: The teacher said, 'The sun rises in the east.'
options: The teacher said that the sun rose in the east.
The teacher said that the sun rises in the east.
The teacher told that the sun rises in the east.
The teacher said the sun rises in the east.
answer: The teacher said that the sun rises in the east.
explanation: Universal truths do not change their tense in indirect speech. ✅

question: Change to Indirect: They said, 'We have won the match.'
options: They said that we have won the match.
They said that they had won the match.
They told that they had won the match.
They said that they won the match.
answer: They said that they had won the match.
explanation: Present Perfect 'have won' changes to Past Perfect 'had won'. 'We' changes to 'they'. ✅

question: Change to Indirect: He said, 'I can do this work.'
options: He said that he could do that work.
He said that he can do that work.
He said that he could do this work.
He told that he can do this work.
answer: He said that he could do that work.

question: Change to Indirect: Sita said to Geeta, 'You are my best friend.'
options: Sita told Geeta that she was my best friend.
Sita told Geeta that you were her best friend.
Sita told Geeta that she was her best friend.
Sita said to Geeta that she was her best friend.
answer: Sita told Geeta that she was her best friend.
explanation: 'You' changes to Geeta (she) and 'my' changes to Sita (her). ✅

question: Change to Indirect: The boys said, 'We were playing cricket.'
options: The boys said that they were playing cricket.
The boys said that they had been playing cricket.
The boys said that we had been playing cricket.
The boys said that they had played cricket.
answer: The boys said that they had been playing cricket.
explanation: Past Continuous 'were playing' changes to Past Perfect Continuous 'had been playing'. ✅

question: Change to Indirect: He said, 'I live here.'
options: He said that he lived there.
He said that he lives here.
He said that he lived here.
He said that he live there.
answer: He said that he lived there.

question: Change to Indirect: My father said, 'Honesty is the best policy.'
options: My father said that honesty was the best policy.
My father said that honesty is the best policy.
My father told that honesty is the best policy.
My father says that honesty is the best policy.
answer: My father said that honesty is the best policy.
explanation: Proverbs and universal truths remain in the same tense. ✅
